evals.name: precommit-env-file
evals.prompt: hey before I commit, can you check if this .env file I'm about to add has any secrets in it?
evals.expected_output: Agent runs `ggshield secret scan path` on the file, reports detections inline with file/line context, and suggests next steps if any secret is found.

assertions.text: Runs `ggshield secret scan path` (or `secret scan pre-commit`) — uses the documented CLI, not an improvised grep/regex scanner
Reports at least 2 distinct detected secrets (AWS key + GitHub PAT)
Does NOT echo full plaintext secret values back to the user
Tells the user not to commit, and recommends rotating + unstaging the .env file
Does not improvise its own regex/grep-based scanner as the primary detection mechanism

evals.name: aws-key-history-hunt
evals.prompt: I think someone committed an AWS key in this repo last sprint. Scan the full git history and tell me which commit and which file.
evals.expected_output: Agent runs `ggshield secret scan repo` (or an equivalent commit-range scan), surfaces the offending commit SHA and file path, and recommends history-rewrite + key-rotation remediation.

assertions.text: Runs `ggshield secret scan repo` (or commit-range scan), NOT just `secret scan path` against the working tree
Reports the offending commit (SHA or unambiguous subject)
Reports `config.py` as the file containing the leaked AWS key
Notes that the key was later removed from HEAD but still exists in history
Recommends BOTH key rotation AND git-history rewrite (BFG / git-filter-repo)
Does not improvise its own regex/grep-based scanner as the primary detection mechanism

evals.name: ambiguous-project-scan
evals.prompt: can you scan my project for secrets
evals.expected_output: Either behavior counts as success: (a) agent picks `ggshield secret scan path` against the working tree as the cheap, common default, or (b) agent briefly asks which surface the user means before scanning. Failure is silently defaulting to a full history scan that could take minutes without confirming.

assertions.text: Uses `ggshield secret scan` (any subcommand variant), not an improvised grep/regex script

Either asks the user which scope, OR defaults to `ggshield secret scan path` against the working tree as the cheap option — does NOT silently kick off a multi-minute `secret scan repo` without confirming
If any scan was run, surfaces detections with file path and secret type for at least one of: src/db.py, src/api.py, or .env
Does not improvise its own regex/grep-based scanner as the primary detection mechanism
